items.headline: Philippines declares nationwide fuel rationing as refineries halt operations
items.body: Manila imposed household and transport fuel limits after its two main refineries suspended processing due to crude supply disruptions. The rationing affects 115 million people and marks the country's first peacetime energy emergency since independence.

items.headline: India and China agree to joint patrols in disputed border zone
items.body: Both militaries will conduct coordinated movements in Ladakh's Depsang Plains following talks in New Delhi. The arrangement reverses four years of separate deployments after the 2020 clash that killed dozens.

items.headline: European Central Bank cuts interest rates as inflation falls below target
items.body: The bank lowered its benchmark rate for the third consecutive month as consumer prices dropped. The move signals confidence that energy shocks have passed and prioritises growth over price stability.

Majority of young South Koreans now support abolishing gender equality ministry

es.headline: Foreign investment in Spain falls by a fifth to lowest level since 2021
es.body: Direct investment dropped to €30.76 billion in 2025 while global flows grew, widening the gap with other advanced economies. The decline cuts across sectors and regions as business confidence weakens.

uk.headline: Welfare reform cuts health benefits for new claimants while removing two-child limit
uk.body: Universal Credit changes reduce monthly health payments to 217 pounds for most new disabled claimants from 423 pounds, while all families now receive child support regardless of family size. The government says the rebalancing will remove work disincentives and save nearly one billion pounds annually.

uk.headline: Sick pay now available from first day of illness for all workers
uk.body: Statutory sick pay becomes payable from day one instead of day four, and the earnings threshold is removed to extend coverage to all employees. The reforms make 1.3 million more workers eligible and increase costs for employers across sectors with part-time and lower-paid staff.
